calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Off Calendar

Pltf Emporium Mall Notice Of And Request For Prove Up Hearing For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for request for prove-up hearing for entry of judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:23:06 p.m. and concluding at 02:38:54 p.m.

Plaintiff Emporium Mall, a Delaware Limited Liability Company represented by Kristine L. Craig, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Elton Kapuniai not appearing.

KEN GILMAN is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
KEN GILMAN is examined by the Court. Witness attests that its Declaration filed on Feb-10-2023 with attached police reports is true and correct.

Court determines the following:

1. Proof of Service filed as to Defendant Elton Kapuniai on Mar-11-2022
2. Default against Defendant Elton Kapuniai was entered on Feb-10-2023
3. There are no DOE Defendants

The matter is submitted.
Based on the documents filed, the statements made by Plaintiff's witness and its Counsel, Declaration of Ken Gilman, the Court finds Plaintiff has established causes of action alleged in the operative complaint. Defendant is a nuisance and trespasser. Plaintiff's request for injunctive relief is GRANTED.

After consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Emporium Mall, a Delaware Limited Liability Company, and against Defendant Elton Kapuniai, as follows:

1. Defendant Elton Kapuniai is enjoined and restrained from entering the Westfield San Francisco Centre, located at: 845 and 865 Market Street, San Francisco, California, for a period of three years expiring May 10, 2026 at 9:00 a.m.

Proposed judgment to be submitted.

Plaintiff Emporium Mall Llc'S Application For Preliminary Injunction [Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 2/2/2022]
Off Calendar Feb-14-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Kristine Craig, Esq. (415.777.3200; kcraig@hansonbridgett.com) of Hanson Bridgett LLP for Plaintiff/moving party.
No other appearances.

Discussed (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). Ms. Craig stated they were unable to serve the homeless defendant and requested a continuance.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Emporium Mall LLC's Application For Preliminary Injunction is taken off calendar. Moving party to file a new motion to get it back on calendar when they are able to serve the defendant.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
